hard.next_step: Confidence 50% 30th Jun Reach out to Wheelson for update/status- start building direct relationship with Mumtalkat We are yet to meet prospect and only demo'd to Wheelson (2 times) and are shortlisted to us and Aerchain - activity has slowed due to geo political issues, expect to resume next week- we MUST take control of cycle moving forward- 3rd June - Spoke to Wheelson CEO, no update from Mumtalakat, gulf situation means project likely delayed by a few weeks, anticipating resumption/next steps ~late June. 15th April - Caught up with CEO of Wheelson for project update: Details are: Confirmed the client is Mumtalaket - the Bahrani Sovereign Wealth Fund Zycus has been shortlisted for phase 2 alongside Aerchain for the 'Enterprise' project track (and Zip for the 'Small' project track) The war has caused some delay in the project, but expecting confirmation and clarification of next steps by mid May Anticipated next step(s) will be a 1-2 day workshop in Bahrain demoing to stakeholders in the pilot organization Initial plan was a paid proof of concept project with an enterprise pilot - prior to a roll-out across the group, not clear at this stage if such a roll-out will be mandated or optional - there are c60 entities of various sizes/shareholding - including: Gulf Air, Aluminum Bahrain, Batelco Group, McLaren Group, Nat Bank of Bahrain. No immediate follow up actions until Mumtalakat/Wheelson confirm the way forward. 18th Mar - Final overview demo focussing on Sourcing, with Sourcing SMEs in attendance, next steps: Wheelson to prepare a detailed SWAT-based comparative summary of all 5 vendors for the client’s two‑day workshop in the second week of April, aiming to narrow from 5 to 2 vendors. Reach out to us over email with sharp / specific follow‑up questions or requests for deeper dives in particular areas, if needed before the April workshop. If Zycus is shortlisted into the next stage, come back to us for support on a more detailed evaluation, including a potential pilot/POC for one entity. Zycus provide additional clarifications, demos, or artefacts (e.g., screenshots like AI scoring, bid normalization details) whenever requested by Samynathan to support their internal analysis and April workshop. Be on standby for a deeper workshop or paid POC if Zycus is selected into the final 2–3 vendors in the long‑term evaluation phase 16th Mar - Initial overview of Zycus I2O (excluding CLM - out of scope), plus live demo, sourcing drill-down to follow as next step - scheduled for 18th Mar 9th Mar - Intro overview call with CEO ..... client is confidential, but is a similar description to the Bahraini Sovereign Wealth fund: Mumtalakat Holding Company . Situation • Wheelson (consultancy / solutions integrator) is advising a Bahrain-based investment company (c. $20bn AUM, 60 entities) on selecting a comprehensive, end-to-end digital procurement platform. • They currently use highly customized, point AI/GenAI solutions across multiple business areas (incl. procurement), which are hard to replicate or scale across customers. • For this client, they started with point solutions for tender management, supplier intelligence, and P2P automation for some mid-scale entities; engagement has deepened to an overall consulting mandate. Impact / Pain • Existing work led to fragmented, non‑repeatable solutions and difficulty scaling products or reusing them across entities. • Manufacturing/other sectors were slow to adopt AI; they’re looking for a domain where AI value is adopted more aggressively – hence pivot to procurement. • The client has 12+ years of supplier data but essentially no external supplier intelligence and wants an “intelligent compilation” of internal + external data for supplier choice and relationship management. • Procurement is treated as strategic, centrally governed from HQ across 60 entities, with hybrid central/decentral models, making governance, intake experience, and guided buying critical. (Proposed) Resolution • Wheelson is running a structured evaluation of “high edge” enterprise S2P suites; Zycus and Jaggaer are the final two contenders to complete initial evaluation. • Plan is: initial generic capabilities evaluation now (no NDA), then for 2 shortlisted vendors a deeper NDA-based workshop paid POC/POV for one entity, then scale to multiple entities if successful. • Next step for Zycus specifically: show end-to-end “demand to PO” process (excluding CLM), strong intake & guided buying, supplier intelligence , sourcing, and ability to build /extend AI agents (Merlin, orchestration, etc.) without heavy OEM dependence.
hard.ais_why:
• Per the 18 Mar 2026 rep note: 'Final overview demo focussing on Sourcing, with Sourcing SMEs in attendance... Wheelson to prepare a detailed SWAT-based comparative summary of all 5 vendors for the client's two-day workshop in the second week of April, aiming to narrow from 5 to 2 vendors.' The buyer (Mumtalakat via Wheelson) is running a structured SWAT vendor compare focused on Sourcing - AI/Merlin not the lead criterion in any quoted moment.
• Per the 15 Apr 2026 rep note: 'Zycus has been shortlisted for phase 2 alongside Aerchain for the Enterprise project track (and Zip for the Small project track)... Anticipated next step(s) will be a 1-2 day workshop in Bahrain demoing to stakeholders in the pilot organization.' Shortlisting against Aerchain (an autonomous sourcing AI vendor) signals AI is in the mix, but no Mumtalakat stakeholder is named or quoted on AI specifically.
• Medium confidence: Avoma returned zero meetings for this opp (correctly - Wheelson is the consultant lead). Products in scope include Merlin Intake + ANA which suggests AI relevance, and the competitive set (Aerchain) confirms AI is evaluated. But no named buyer quote about Merlin/agentic/autonomous exists in rep notes. Conservative AI Curious.

north_star_verdict.headline: Partner-led Enterprise finalist (vs Aerchain/JAGGAER) paused by a genuine gulf-geopolitical delay, not deprioritisation; last real buyer reply was Samynathan on 4 Jun saying phase-2 evaluation had not started, the Bahrain workshop and paid POC are both unscheduled, and the owner just changed from Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim, so the 28 Aug close is not credible without a confirmed restart in the next 2-3 weeks.
north_star_verdict.math: Stage Shortlisted since 15 Apr 2026 (~82 days in stage); ~103 days in cycle since 25 Mar. Close 2026-08-28 is 53 days out. Last genuine two-way buyer touch was Samynathan's inbound email 2026-06-04 (32 days ago); everything since (25 May, 1 Jun, 2 Jul) is rep-side outreach awaiting reply. The NDA workshop and paid POC both sit ahead of close and neither is scheduled, so the date is not defensible without a confirmed stage-2 restart.
evidence: Next_Step_History__c 2026-06-03: Wheelson CEO reported no update from Mumtalakat, the gulf situation means the project is likely delayed by a few weeks, with resumption anticipated late June (source: SF Next_Step__c).
Buyer email received 2026-06-04: Samynathan wrote the planned second level of evaluation had not started and proposed reconnecting mid-June (source: SF Task 2026-06-04, Clari email received).
Owner reassigned 2026-06-29 from Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim (new local RVP), who emailed Samynathan on 2026-07-02 to pick Phase 2 back up (source: SF field history 2026-06-29; SF Task 2026-07-02).

Best Case forecast on $300k with no Mumtalakat economic buyer mapped, no direct client contact ever made, and no commercial/pricing conversation logged (source: MEDDPICC 2.0, SF Amount/ForecastCategory, Next_Step__c 2026-06-30).

metrics.narrative: The business problem is articulated clearly but never quantified. Per the 9 Mar 2026 intro-call log, Mumtalakat has 12+ years of internal supplier data but essentially no external supplier intelligence, fragmented point solutions across ~60 entities, and no end-to-end digital source-to-pay platform with structured intake and guided buying (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 9 Mar 2026). No cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings figure has been stated by anyone at Mumtalakat, and no ROI model exists, partly because Zycus has never spoken with the client directly. Quantifying the value of supplier intelligence and intake automation for the single pilot entity is the open move that would harden the value case.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Mumtalakat budget owner is identified by name and none can be inferred honestly, because every conversation to date has been with the Wheelson consultancy, not the client. MEDDPICC 2.0 records the budget owner generically as 'Mumtalakat' with the economic buyer marked TBC, and the 30 Jun Next_Step confirms Zycus is yet to meet the prospect and has only demoed to Wheelson (source: MEDDPICC 2.0; SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-30). As a c.$20bn sovereign wealth fund running a pilot ahead of a possible 60-entity rollout, sign-off almost certainly runs through an investment committee or board whose chain and thresholds are unknown. The path to power runs THROUGH the champion, so arming Samynathan to broker the first direct Mumtalakat sponsor introduction is the top move, not cold outreach into the fund.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are documented from the evaluation structure Wheelson ran: an end-to-end 'demand to PO' process excluding CLM, strong intake and guided buying, supplier intelligence combining 12 years of internal data with external sources, sourcing depth, and the ability to build and extend AI agents (Merlin, orchestration) without heavy OEM dependence (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 9 Mar and 18 Mar 2026). The 18 Mar demo used a SWAT-based comparative summary across all five vendors to narrow from five to two (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-03-18). These are confirmed at the partner level but have not been validated directly with Mumtalakat's own stakeholders, so the weighting each client sponsor applies is still inferred.

decision_process.narrative: A documented two-stage motion: Wheelson runs the vendor evaluation (overview demos 16 and 18 Mar, then a two-day client workshop in the second week of April) to narrow five vendors to two, after which the two shortlisted vendors enter a deeper NDA-based workshop and a paid POC/POV for one entity before any scale to multiple entities (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 9 Mar, 18 Mar and 15 Apr 2026). Zycus cleared the first gate into the Enterprise-track final alongside Aerchain (source: SF Next_Step__c 15 Apr 2026). The process is currently paused by the gulf geopolitical situation, with resumption and a Bahrain stakeholder workshop anticipated but not yet scheduled.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown. The only structure logged is a planned NDA ahead of the deeper workshop and a paid POC/POV for one entity before scaling (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 9 Mar 2026). No MSA, SOW, procurement channel, InfoSec process, or commercial terms between Zycus and Wheelson are documented, and it is not clear whether Wheelson contracts as reseller/SI or Zycus contracts directly with the relevant stakeholder a sovereign wealth fund the approval and procurement path likely runs through an investment committee, but it is unmapped. Confirming the paid-POC scope and the NDA sequence in writing, and naming who owns paper on the Zycus side, would create the first paper-process visibility.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly identified, owned by Mumtalakat and surfaced via Wheelson: fragmented, non-repeatable point solutions across ~60 entities that are hard to scale; no external supplier intelligence despite 12 years of internal data; no structured intake and guided buying; and a hybrid central/decentral governance model that makes governance and guided buying critical (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 9 Mar 2026). The strategic driver is a deliberate pivot to procurement as the domain where AI value can be adopted more aggressively than in their manufacturing sectors. The pain is confirmed and well aligned to Zycus strengths, but it remains the partner's read of the client and has not yet been validated in a direct conversation with a Mumtalakat pain owner.

champion.narrative: Samynathan R., Co-Founder and CEO of Wheelson Technologies, is the primary and only champion: he arranged the intro call, had done prior research on Merlin, authored the SWAT evaluation structure, and controls the vendor selection process (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 Champion; SF OpportunityContactRole). He is an active, well-informed advocate and the genuine path to power, but he is a consultancy champion, not an internal Mumtalakat sponsor, which makes him simultaneously the deal's greatest strength and its single point of failure. What is still missing is any direct Mumtalakat advocate and any two-way engagement between Zycus and the fund itself, so if Samynathan's influence wanes the deal goes blind. His last genuine reply was 4 Jun; the new owner Mohamad Alhakim must rebuild that relationship quickly.

competition.narrative: Zycus is a finalist in the Enterprise track of a structured five-to-two down-select. The 9 Mar log named Zycus and JAGGAER as the final two contenders to complete initial evaluation, with Coupa, iValua, GEP and SAP Ariba also in the original field, all of which were narrowed out at the April workshop (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 9 Mar 2026). The 15 Apr update names Aerchain alongside Zycus on the Enterprise track and Zip on the separate Small track (source: SF Next_Step_History__c 15 Apr 2026). JAGGAER and Aerchain are the live peers; the wedge is Merlin agentic extensibility (build/orchestrate agents without OEM lock-in) plus external supplier intelligence combining internal 12-year data with external sources, none of which has been validated in front of Mumtalakat yet because the deeper NDA workshop has not happened.

deal_movement.summary: The deal is paused by the gulf geopolitical situation with no client-side movement since April, while the Zycus side changed hands: Dan Quinn left and the new local RVP Mohamad Alhakim took ownership on 29 Jun and reopened contact with Samynathan on 2 Jul. The last genuine buyer touch was Samynathan's 4 Jun reply that stage-2 evaluation had not started.

items.change: New local RVP Mohamad Alhakim emailed Samynathan to restart Phase 2, flagging Dan Quinn's departure from Zycus.
Ownership reassigned from Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim by Carl Kimball.
Samynathan replied that the planned second-level evaluation had not started and proposed a mid-June reconnect.
Wheelson CEO reported the gulf situation would likely delay the project a few weeks, with resumption anticipated late June.
Zycus shortlisted for Phase 2 on the Enterprise track alongside Aerchain; stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted, forecast to Best Case.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is a finalist in the Enterprise-track down-select; the live current threats are JAGGAER (named final-two contender on 9 Mar and still in Competitors__c) and Aerchain (the AI sourcing vendor named alongside Zycus for the Enterprise track on 15 Apr). Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua and SAP Ariba were all narrowed out at the April workshop and are now dormant; Zip is on the separate Small track and does not compete with Zycus. The single strongest current threat is Aerchain, an autonomous-sourcing AI point player pitched directly against our Merlin story, because capabilities are close and the differentiating Merlin plus supplier-intelligence wedge has not yet been validated in front of Mumtalakat.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Mumtalakat's stated criteria map well to the Zycus suite, but every criterion is confirmed only through Wheelson and none has been validated directly with the client, so fit is strong-on-paper and exposed-on-proof.

best_practice_check.summary: The win levers now are breaking out of a 100% partner-mediated channel, differentiating hard before the Bahrain workshop, and protecting relationship continuity through the owner change.
flags: Break single-threading: every contact is Wheelson and Zycus has never met a Mumtalakat stakeholder; use the new-RVP transition as a natural reason for a direct client introduction through Samynathan.

Differentiate before the Bahrain workshop: pre-arm Samynathan with the Merlin agent-extensibility and supplier-intelligence wedge against Aerchain (AI point player) and JAGGAER, since capabilities are close and the decision could revert to price or brand.
Protect continuity through the owner change: Dan Quinn's departure risks a relationship reset, so Mohamad Alhakim should re-establish trust and cadence with Samynathan before Phase 2 resumes.

items.name: Samynathan R.
items.title: Co-Founder & CEO
items.risk: Partner-side champion who authored the evaluation and controls the process; the path to power but also the sole channel, and Zycus has no direct Mumtalakat relationship if his influence wanes.

items.title: Head of Delivery and Solutioning

champion_strength.summary: Samynathan R., Wheelson's Co-Founder and CEO, is an active, well-informed partner champion who arranged the intro, researched Merlin, and authored the SWAT evaluation, but he is a consultancy champion rather than an internal Mumtalakat advocate, which is both the deal's strength and its single point of failure; his last genuine reply was 4 Jun and the owner has since changed.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin Intake and ANA are in scope and the differentiation story (build/extend AI agents without OEM lock-in) is the intended wedge against Aerchain and JAGGAER, but it has only been shown to Wheelson, never validated with a Mumtalakat stakeholder, so the AI story is currently under-positioned to the actual buyer.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 5 / Moderate: the buyer is running an AI-forward procurement pivot and shortlisted an AI sourcing vendor (Aerchain), but no named Mumtalakat stakeholder has spoken about Merlin or agentic AI directly, so appetite is inferred from the competitive set and product scope rather than confirmed on a call.

items.detail: Gulf geopolitical situation has paused the project and pushed the phase-2 restart from May to an unconfirmed late-June/July window.
No Mumtalakat economic buyer or any client-side stakeholder has ever engaged Zycus; the entire deal is read second-hand through Wheelson.
The deal runs 100% through Wheelson as a single channel, so a stall or shift in the consultancy's priorities stalls the whole opportunity.
The 28 Aug close is not credible: the NDA workshop and paid POC both sit ahead of it and neither is scheduled.
Owner change mid-cycle (Dan Quinn left Zycus; Mohamad Alhakim took over 29 Jun) risks a relationship reset with the champion just as Phase 2 should restart.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: the deal has been paused since April by the geopolitical delay, the last genuine buyer reply was 4 Jun, and recent activity is entirely rep-side outreach through an owner transition awaiting a response.
items: Last genuine two-way buyer touch was Samynathan's inbound email on 4 Jun; 25 May, 1 Jun and 2 Jul are all rep-initiated nudges.
Phase-2 evaluation has not started and its restart is unconfirmed (source: SF Task 2026-06-04).

items.action: Mohamad Alhakim to secure a firm Phase-2 restart date from Samynathan and a direct Mumtalakat intro.
items.trigger: New RVP reopened contact 2 Jul; last buyer reply 4 Jun said evaluation had not started.
items.expected_effect: Converts a paused, partner-only deal into a scheduled workshop and first direct client relationship, restoring a credible path to close.

items.action: Arm Samynathan with a Merlin plus supplier-intelligence differentiation pack for the Bahrain workshop.
items.trigger: Finalist vs Aerchain and JAGGAER with the AI/supplier-intelligence wedge unvalidated with the client.
items.expected_effect: Sharpens the win theme before the down-select so the decision does not revert to price or brand.

items.action: Confirm the paid-POC scope and NDA sequence for the pilot entity in writing.
items.trigger: Only a planned NDA and paid POC/POV for one entity are logged; no scope or paper documented.
items.expected_effect: Creates the first paper-process visibility and a concrete forcing step toward Vendor Selected.

items.action: Quantify supplier-intelligence and intake ROI for one pilot entity to build the value case.
items.trigger: Pain is confirmed but no cost, cycle-time or savings figure has been stated by the client.
items.expected_effect: Gives the eventual economic buyer a defensible ROI story and supports the Best Case forecast.

critical_signals.text: The last genuine buyer reply was Samynathan on 4 June saying the second-level evaluation had not started and the project was delayed by the gulf situation.
Dan Quinn has left Zycus and new local RVP Mohamad Alhakim took the deal over on 29 June, emailing Samynathan on 2 July to restart Phase 2 - a relationship still to be re-established.

Zycus is a finalist on the Enterprise track against Aerchain and JAGGAER, but the differentiating Merlin and supplier-intelligence wedge has never been shown to the actual client, only to the Wheelson consultancy.
It is a $300k Best Case with no client-side budget owner ever engaged and no pricing or ROI conversation held, so the number is not yet defensible.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is an Enterprise-track finalist that Wheelson shortlisted to the final two, but we have never met the actual buyer, the deal is paused by a genuine gulf-geopolitical delay, and the owner just changed hands - so the win read holds mid-band and the whole thing hinges on Samynathan brokering a direct Mumtalakat restart.

win_position.text: Scored mid-band, not high, because although we cleared the five-to-two Enterprise down-select (source: Next_Step 15 Apr), Zycus has never spoken to a single Mumtalakat stakeholder and the economic buyer is unmapped (source: Next_Step 30 Jun).

We are one of two Enterprise-track finalists alongside Aerchain, with strong criteria fit on end-to-end source-to-pay, supplier intelligence and Merlin agent extensibility (source: Next_Step_History 9 Mar and 15 Apr).

The champion is partner-side only: Samynathan controls the evaluation but is a Wheelson consultant, not an internal Mumtalakat advocate, so the deal goes blind if his influence wanes (source: OpportunityContactRole; MEDDPICC 2.0).

The differentiating AI and supplier-intelligence wedge against Aerchain and JAGGAER has never been validated with the client, so a close decision could revert to price or brand (source: Next_Step_History 15 Apr).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has stalled since April: the project is paused by the gulf situation and the phase-2 restart, first anticipated for May then late June, is still unconfirmed (source: Next_Step 15 Apr and 3 Jun).
The only recent activity is rep-side outreach through an owner transition (25 May, 1 Jun, 2 Jul); the last genuine buyer reply was 4 June (source: SF Tasks).

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is partner-mediated: Wheelson advanced Zycus to the final two, but no Mumtalakat stakeholder has committed a date, budget or next meeting, and Zycus has never met the client (source: Next_Step 30 Jun).
Samynathan continues to represent the deal actively and confirmed on 4 June he is following up with the prospect on the evaluation restart (source: SF Task 2026-06-04).

deal_risk.text: The 28 Aug close is not credible with the NDA workshop and paid POC both unscheduled, so Best Case is not defensible and Pipeline is the honest category (source: CloseDate; Next_Step_History).

Single-channel and owner-change risk compound: the deal runs 100% through Wheelson and just changed Zycus owners, so a stall in either relationship stalls everything (source: field history 29 Jun; OpportunityContactRole).

close_plan_concretizing.evidence: Phase-2 restart, workshop and POC all unscheduled and paused by the geopolitical delay (Next_Step 3 Jun).

seniority_rising.evidence: No Mumtalakat executive has ever engaged; all contact is via the Wheelson consultancy (Next_Step 30 Jun).

scope_change.from: Enterprise pilot (paid POC on one entity) ahead of a possible ~60-entity rollout
scope_change.to: Enterprise pilot (paid POC on one entity) ahead of a possible ~60-entity rollout
scope_change.detail: Scope is unchanged: full source-to-pay suite (iSource, iSupplier, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA) with amount raised from $250k to $300k on 25 Mar; the pilot-then-scale structure is the original plan, not a reduction.

customer_preference.evidence: Wheelson down-selected Zycus into the Enterprise-track final two (Next_Step 15 Apr), a positive third-party signal, but no direct Mumtalakat preference has been expressed and Zycus has never met the client.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain is well articulated (no external supplier intelligence, fragmented point solutions across ~60 entities, no end-to-end S2P) but no cost, cycle-time or savings figure has been quantified (Next_Step_History 9 Mar).

reasons.detail: Wheelson Technologies is a $300k Best Case, forecasted enterprise deal in a two-horse race vs JAGGAER, but the 30 Jun next-step admits activity has slowed and Zycus has still only demo'd to Wheelson (the consultancy) twice and never met the end client Mumtalakat. The economic buyer is a gap (no named budget owner) and there has been no update from Mumtalakat since the 3 Jun call with the Wheelson CEO. If this drift continues, a forecasted six-figure deal reverts to price or brand against JAGGAER with no internal Mumtalakat sponsor to defend Zycus.
reasons.metric: $300k Best Case; momentum 48; economic buyer = gap; 0 direct meetings with Mumtalakat
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Mohamad why a $300k Best-Case deal has zero direct contact with Mumtalakat and what the concrete stage-2 restart trigger and date are, given activity 'slowed' per the 30 Jun note.
reasons.summary: $300k forecasted deal going soft: only demo'd the consultancy, no word from the real buyer.
reasons.evidence: Next step 30 Jun: 'We are yet to meet prospect and only demo'd to Wheelson (2 times)... activity has slowed due to geo political issues, expect to resume next week - we MUST take control of cycle moving forward - 3rd June - Spoke to Wheelson CEO, no update from Mumtalakat'.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 50 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 50 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 25 is anchored on the stage win (50), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: Zycus is an Enterprise-track finalist that Wheelson shortlisted to the final two, but we have never met the actual buyer, the deal is paused by a genuine gulf-geopolitical delay, and the owner just changed hands - so the win read holds mid-band and the whole thing hinges on Samynathan brokering a direct Mumtalakat restart.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Scored mid-band, not high, because although we cleared the five-to-two Enterprise down-select (source: Next_Step 15 Apr), Zycus has never spoken to a single Mumtalakat stakeholder and the economic buyer is unmapped (source: Next_Step 30 Jun).

We are one of two Enterprise-track finalists alongside Aerchain, with strong criteria fit on end-to-end source-to-pay, supplier intelligence and Merlin agent extensibility (source: Next_Step_History 9 Mar and 15 Apr).

The champion is partner-side only: Samynathan controls the evaluation but is a Wheelson consultant, not an internal Mumtalakat advocate, so the deal goes blind if his influence wanes (source: OpportunityContactRole; MEDDPICC 2.0).

The differentiating AI and supplier-intelligence wedge against Aerchain and JAGGAER has never been validated with the client, so a close decision could revert to price or brand (source: Next_Step_History 15 Apr).

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 50.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Momentum has stalled since April: the project is paused by the gulf situation and the phase-2 restart, first anticipated for May then late June, is still unconfirmed (source: Next_Step 15 Apr and 3 Jun).
The only recent activity is rep-side outreach through an owner transition (25 May, 1 Jun, 2 Jul); the last genuine buyer reply was 4 June (source: SF Tasks).

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: The 28 Aug close is not credible with the NDA workshop and paid POC both unscheduled, so Best Case is not defensible and Pipeline is the honest category (source: CloseDate; Next_Step_History).

Single-channel and owner-change risk compound: the deal runs 100% through Wheelson and just changed Zycus owners, so a stall in either relationship stalls everything (source: field history 29 Jun; OpportunityContactRole).

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Zycus is a finalist in the Enterprise-track down-select; the live current threats are JAGGAER (named final-two contender on 9 Mar and still in Competitors__c) and Aerchain (the AI sourcing vendor named alongside Zycus for the Enterprise…

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Commitment is partner-mediated: Wheelson advanced Zycus to the final two, but no Mumtalakat stakeholder has committed a date, budget or next meeting, and Zycus has never met the client (source: Next_Step 30 Jun).
Samynathan continues to represent the deal actively and confirmed on 4 June he is following up with the prospect on the evaluation restart (source: SF Task 2026-06-04).

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Mohamad Alhakim to secure a firm Phase-2 restart date from Samynathan and a direct Mumtalakat intro. (by 13 Jul)
Arm Samynathan with a Merlin plus supplier-intelligence differentiation pack for the Bahrain workshop. (by 20 Jul)
Confirm the paid-POC scope and NDA sequence for the pilot entity in writing. (by 22 Jul)

gaps: Zero Avoma calls exist for this opportunity; the buyer relationship is entirely partner-mediated through Wheelson, so all evidence is Next_Step_History, tasks and field history rather than direct call transcripts.
No Mumtalakat client-side stakeholder, economic buyer, or decision maker has ever been named or engaged.
No quantified ROI / value case has been stated by the client.
